ogTitle: Victory Heights vs Villeneuve: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Victory Heights and Villeneuve.
heading: Victory Heights vs Villeneuve — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Victory Heights, Queensland and Villeneuve, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Victory Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Villeneuve (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Victory Heights or Villeneuve safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Victory Heights has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Villeneuve scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Victory Heights scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Villeneuve scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Victory Heights or Villeneuve?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Victory Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.53% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
